topic.what: Privacy-preserving approximate nearest neighbor search using hybrid FHE+TEE with product quantization
topic.how: Combines CKKS-based FHE with TEE, employs optimized data packings (SDOP/SDRP/WOP/WRP), and proposes faster summation algorithm for distance computation on ciphertexts
topic.why_matters: Enables practical privacy-preserving vector search for LLM/VLM applications while protecting against embedding inversion and membership attacks on highly confidential data

characteristics.what_it_measures: Server-side queries per second during sequential search
characteristics.evidence: Table 9: GloVe-200d achieves 55.7 QPS with optimal parameters
characteristics.context: Achieved >50 QPS when multiplicative depth=1, ds>1, NW_OP=1

characteristics.what_it_measures: Hit rate for top-10 nearest neighbor retrieval
characteristics.evidence: Table 9: SIFT achieves 100% Recall@10, GloVe-100d achieves 97.9%
characteristics.context: High recall achievable with large ns and small NC parameter settings

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total time for codebook generation, encoding, and indexing
characteristics.evidence: Abstract and evaluation: SIFT 1M completes within 2 hours
characteristics.context: Includes codebook generation, database encoding, and index generation for 1M-scale datasets

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ciphertext traffic between client and server per query
characteristics.evidence: Table 9: Ranges from 1.0 MiB (GIST/GloVe) to 6.0 MiB (SIFT LCP)
characteristics.context: Reduced through optimized WOP/WRP data packing

characteristics.what_it_measures: Database encoding speed for indexing data
characteristics.evidence: Table 7: GloVe-25d achieves 2265.6 QPS with SCP
characteristics.context: Scales with ns and NC parameter choices

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean squared error between encoded and original data
characteristics.evidence: Table 6: GIST with 320 subspaces achieves MSE=0.7; SIFT achieves MSE=4400
characteristics.context: Trade-off between MSE and computational efficiency
